data.title: Affirm Holdings issues strong FY2026 guidance after delivering Q4 earnings, revenue beat
data.description:
Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ:AFRM) stock climbed 9.6% in Thursday after-hours trading after the company's gross merchandise volume logged strong gains in fiscal Q4, helping to deliver an earnings and revenue beat. With that momentum, it issued solid guidance for FY2026 and Q1.

The buy now, pay later lending platform expects FY2026 gross merchandise volume of more than $46B (Visible Alpha consensus $45.7B). Its outlook for full-year revenue is ~8.4% of GMV, or ~$3.86B ($3.89B consensus). Adjusted operating margin is seen at more than 26.1% vs. 24.1% in FY 2025.

Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) expects Q1 revenue of $855M-$885M vs. $858.4M consensus; and GMV of $10.1B-$10.4 vs. Visible Alpha estimate of $9.83B.  Adjusted operating margin is expected to be 23%-25% vs. 27.0% in Q4 2025.

Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.20, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.11, climbed from $0.01 in Q3 and -$0.14 in last year’s Q4.

Total net revenue of $876.4M for the quarter ended June 30, 2025, beating the $837.1M consensus, increased from $783.1M in the prior quarter and $659.2M in the year-ago period.

GMV volume climbed to $10.4B from $8.6B in Q3 and $7.2B in last year’s Q4, exceeding the $9.64B Visible Alpha consensus. The Y/Y growth was driven by the strength with its largest merchant partners, 0% APR monthly installment loans, and the direct-to-consumer business, including Affirm Card. 

Q4 Affirm (AFRM) Card attach rate reached 10%. Card GMV more than doubled to $1.2B, and active cardholders jumped surged 97% to 2.3M, the company said.

The number of active consumers, excluding the discontinued Returnly business, rose 24% Y/Y to 23.0M at June 30, 2025. 

Adjusted operating margin was 27.0% vs. 22.2% in the prior quarter and 22.7% a year ago.

Funding capacity grew to $26.1B at June, 30, 025, from $23.3B at March 31.

Conference call at 5:00 PM ET.

Earlier, Affirm GAAP EPS of $0.20 beats by $0.09, revenue of $876.42M beats by $39.37M

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Dell guides FY25 above estimates after FQ2 double beat
Gap slides after Athleta weakness leads to a comparable sales miss
data.description:
Gap (NYSE:GAP) traded lower in postmarket trading on Thursday after the retailer missed comparable sales estimates with its second quarter earnings report. 

Comparable sales were up 1% year-over-year to fall short of the consensus estimate for a rise of 1.7%. Store sales were down 1%, while online sales were up 3% from last year's level. The Old Navy comparable sales rose 2% during the quarter to match the consensus expectation, and Gap brand comparable sales were up +4% vs. +3.8% consensus. Comparable sales rose 4% for the Banana Republic chain vs. +0.4% consensus, while Athleta brand sales were down 9% vs. -5.8% consensus. The company said the Athleta brand will continue to focus on resetting for the long term and improving its product and marketing, which it noted will take time.

Gross margin was 41.2% of sales, vs. 39.8% a year ago and 41.9% consensus. Merchandise margin increased 150 basis points versus last year, primarily driven by lapping the benefit of incremental sales in the second quarter of fiscal 2024 relating to the company's revenue sharing agreement with its credit card partner. Rent, occupancy, and depreciation as a percent of sales leveraged 10 basis points versus last year.

Gap (NYSE:GAP) reported net income of $216 million vs. $206 million a year ago and EPS came in at $0.57 vs. $0.54 a year ago and the consensus mark of $0.55.

Gap (GAP) ended the quarter with a cash position of $2.4 billion and inventory of $2.3 billion (+9% Y/Y). Capital expenditures were $181 million during the quarter.

CEO update: "In the second quarter, Gap Inc. overdelivered on profit expectations and achieved our topline goals. With positive comps for the sixth consecutive quarter, fueled by our three largest brands Old Navy, Gap and Banana Republic, it's clear our strategy is working... We are advancing our transformation with discipline, clarity, and momentum and remain committed to building a high-performing company that delivers sustainable, long-term value for our shareholders."

On the guidance front, Gap (GAP) sees full-year net sales growth of 1% to 2% and an operating margin of 6.7% to 7.0%, including an estimated 100 to 110 basis points of net tariff impact.

Shares of Gap (GAP) slid 7.7% in postmarket trading after the earnings release after falling 2.8% during the regular session.

data.title: Marvell slumps as Q2 results mirror estimates and outlook falls short
data.description:
Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) sank 7% during early post-market trading after it released its second quarter fiscal 2026 financial results.

For the quarter ended August 2, the semiconductor firm reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.67, which mirrored the consensus estimate of $0.67. Revenue for the quarter totaled $2.006B, which just missed the estimate of $2.01B.

Second quarter revenue jumped a staggering 58% year over year. The gains were led by its data center segment, which surged 69% year over year to $1.49B. 

For the quarter in progress, Marvell expects a net revenue midpoint of $2.06B, which is less than the $2.11B estimate. Adjusted EPS for the third quarter is forecast at $0.74, which is also less than the estimate of $0.73.

"Marvell's growth is being fueled by strong AI demand for our custom silicon and electro-optics products, as well as a significant increase in the pace of recovery in our enterprise networking and carrier infrastructure end markets," said Marvell CEO Matt Murphy. "Our custom AI design activity is at an all-time high, with the Marvell team now engaged in over 50 new opportunities across more than 10 customers."

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Ulta Beauty raise full year guidance after FQ2 beat
Dell slides even as it ups forecast amid strong AI demand
data.description:
Dell (NYSE:DELL) shares fell 4.5% in extended trading on Thursday, even as the IT giant reported fiscal second-quarter results and guidance that largely topped Wall Street's expectations. 

For the period ending August 1, Dell said it earned an adjusted $2.32 per share on $29.78B in revenue, up 13% year-over-year. Of the company's two units, Client Solutions Group generated $12.5B in revenue, up 1% year-over-year. Dell's Infrastructure Solutions Group delivered $16.8B in revenue for the quarter, up 44% year-over-year. Included in that was a 69% rise in servers and networking revenue at $12.9B. 

Analysts were anticipating adjusted earnings of $2.11 per share on $25.33B in revenue.

“In Q2, we achieved strong top-line results and profitability, reaching record revenue of $29.8 billion,” said Dell CFO Yvonne McGill in a statement. “We delivered another quarter of robust cash generation, with $2.5 billion in cash flow from operations and $1.3 billion in shareholder returns.”

Looking to the fiscal third-quarter, Dell anticipates adjusted earnings of $2.45 per share at the midpoint, while sales are forecast to be between $26.5B and $27.5B. Analysts were anticipating earnings of $2.55 per share on $26.4B in revenue.

For the full-year, it expects to earn $9.55 per share, excluding one-time items, at the midpoint, above the $9.40 per share estimate. Full-year revenue is expected to be between $105B and $109B, with the $107B midpoint above the $105.03B estimate.

Dell COO Jeff Clarke added that the demand for the company's AI solutions remains “exceptional” and the company now expects to generate $20B in AI server revenue in fiscal 2026, up from its prior outlook.

The company will host a conference call at 4:30 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: COOT gets Nasdaq extension to meet equity listing rule
data.description:
• Australian Oilseeds Holdings Limited (NASDAQ:COOT) shares rose after the company said it received a written decision from the Nasdaq Hearings Panel granting an extension to regain compliance with the exchange’s continued listing standards.
  • Extension runs through September 30, 2025, to meet Nasdaq’s $2.5 million minimum stockholders’ equity requirement.
  • The company states that as of the hearing date on July 22, 2025, it had $2.6 million in shareholder equity.
  • Plans to maintain this minimum equity value by converting additional debt and increasing revenues. 
  • If compliance is achieved, company will be subject to a one-year panel monitor.
  • COOT +49.4% after hours to $0.9599.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Regulator raises alarm on Chinese money laundering networks
data.description:
The U.S. Department of the Treasury, in a report on Thursday, raised an alarm on Chinese money laundering networks, noting ~$312B in suspicious transactions.

The unit Financial Crimes Enforcement Network, or FinCEN, analyzed 137,153 Bank Secrecy Act reports filed by financial institutions between January 2020 and December 2024.

The regulator issued an advisory urging financial institutions to be vigilant in detecting the use of such networks by Mexico-based drug cartels.

Related ETFs: Invesco KBW Bank ETF (KBWB), SPDR® S&P Bank ETF (KBE), First Trust Nasdaq Bank ETF (FTXO), SPDR® S&P Regional Banking ETF (KRE), Themes Global Systemically Important Banks ETF (GSIB), Invesco KBW Regional Banking ETF (KBWR), iShares U.S. Regional Banks ETF (IAT), First Trust NASDAQ® ABA Community Bank Index Fund ETF (QABA)

data.title: SOS terminates deposit agreement, announces changes to share capital, direct listing of ordinary shares
data.description:
• SOS Limited (NYSE:SOS) announced on Thursday its plan to terminate the amended and restated deposit agreement, effective September 8, 2025 
  • In connection with the termination, the Company held an extraordinary general meeting of shareholders on August 11, 2025, at which its shareholders approved an increase to the company's authorized share capital, as well as a 150-for-1 share consolidation of its ordinary shares. 
  • The depositary of the company's American depositary receipts will distribute to all holders and beneficial owners of the company's ADRs an updated notification regarding the termination of the ADR facility for the  company's ADSs pursuant to the deposit agreement. 
  • Following the mandatory exchange, the ordinary shares are anticipated to trade directly on the New York Stock Exchange under the current trading symbol "SOS".
  • SOS Limited (NYSE:SOS) shares were up 1.24% in extended trading.

data.title: Autodesk surges after Q2 results, outlook douse expectations
data.description:
Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) shares jumped 10% after releasing its second quarter fiscal 2026 financial results and an outlook that easily topped market estimates.

For the quarter ended July 31, the engineering and design software company reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.62, which was more than the consensus estimate of $2.45.

Revenue for the quarter increased 17% year over year to total $1.76B versus the estimate of $1.72B. Remaining performance obligations jumped 24% year over year to $7.3B compared to the estimate of $5.88B. Adjusted operating margin was also strong at 39% versus the estimate of 37.8%. Free cash flow blossomed to $451M versus $203M one year prior.

Looking ahead, Autodesk projects third quarter adjusted EPS ranging from $2.48 to $2.51, which is more than the estimate of $2.40. Revenue is expected to range from $1.80B to $1.81B, which also surpasses the $1.77B consensus.

"For more than a decade, Autodesk has been at the forefront of innovation — in BIM, SaaS, generative design, and now in generative AI," said Autodesk CEO Andrew Anagnost. "We have been building industry-specific foundation models and products capable of understanding and reasoning about 2D and 3D geometry, design and make data, complex structures, and even physical behavior."
